title: Whispera WIRAID Module Manifest
description: Schema for module.json — defines a WIRAID plugin binary (v2)

params_schema.description: Declares parameters the module needs. Keys are param names used as {params.KEY} in templates.

name.description: Unique module name (snake_case). Used as directory name.

source.description: URL to source repo or release page.

lang.description: Runtime type. Use 'binary' for pre-compiled executables.

entry.description: For go/rust: path to build entry point (e.g. './cmd/server'). Not needed for binary.

platforms.description: Supported platforms. Omit to support all.

wiraid_id.description: Stable public ID used in pair endpoints (/api/wiraid/public/pair/{wiraid_id}). Must be unique across modules.

peer.description: ID of the counterpart module on the other side (server←→client). Enables pair-config exchange.

pair_exports.description: Server-only. Values are templates rendered with server params and pushed to the peer client. Available placeholders: {server_host}, {params.KEY}.

transport.description: Module provides a socks5/tcp proxy upstream.

pre_cmd.description: Commands to run before the main process starts (e.g. 'apply config'). Each item is an argv array. Run sequentially; failure aborts start.

cmd.description: Main process command. First element is the executable. Runs as a long-lived process.

post_cmd.description: Commands to run after the process is stopped (cleanup). Each item is an argv array.

env.description: Extra environment variables. Values support template placeholders.

config_template.description: Config file content template. Written to config_path before start. Supports all placeholders.

config_path.description: Relative path for the rendered config file inside the module dir.

mode.description: fixed: use the port assigned by the engine. stdout_regex: parse port from stdout. file: read port from a file.

pattern.description: Regex with one capture group containing the port number. Required for stdout_regex mode. Example: 'listening on :(\d+)'

file.description: Relative path to the file containing the port number. Required for file mode.

mode.description: How to detect that the module is ready to accept connections. tcp_connect: try to connect to the listen port. stdout_contains: wait for a line containing 'value'. delay: sleep timeout_ms.

value.description: String to look for in stdout. Required for stdout_contains mode.

ParamSchema.description: Declares a single module parameter. Used in templates as {params.NAME}.

required.description: If true and no generator/default and param is not supplied, start will fail.

default.description: Default value used if no generator and param not explicitly set.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the UI.

generator.description:
Auto-generation strategy run at install/enable time:
• random_hex — hex string of 'length' bytes
• random_alnum — alphanumeric string of 'length' chars
• fixed — constant 'value'
• env — value of environment variable 'env'
• self_signed_cert — writes tls.crt + tls.key to module dir, sets param to cert path
• exec — runs 'exec_cmd', applies 'exec_regex' to stdout

length.description: Byte length for random_hex / char length for random_alnum.

exec_cmd.description: Command argv for 'exec' generator. Supports {binary} and {module_dir} placeholders. Multiple params sharing the same exec_cmd run it only once (cached).

exec_regex.description: Regex applied to the combined stdout+stderr of exec_cmd. First matching group (or exec_group) is used as the value.

exec_group.description: Capture group index to use from exec_regex. Default: 1.

URISpec.description: Declares that this module can parse a share-link URI and fill its params automatically. Enables 'paste key → connect' UX.

schemes.description: URI schemes this module handles (without '://'). Examples: ['vless'], ['vmess'], ['hy2', 'hysteria2'], ['naive+https', 'naive+quic'].

style.description: url: standard RFC 3986 URL (vless/trojan/hy2/naive). base64_json: base64-encoded JSON body (vmess).

scheme_to.description: url style: store the scheme (after stripping any 'naive+' prefix) into this param. Useful for naive transport selection (https/quic/http).

userinfo_to.description: url style: store the full userinfo (user:pass or just token/uuid) into this param.

userinfo_user_to.description: url style: store only the username part of userinfo into this param.

userinfo_pass_to.description: url style: store only the password part of userinfo into this param.

host_to.description: url style: store the hostname into this param. Used for server_host (also drives bypass logic).

port_to.description: url style: store the port number into this param.

path_to.description: url style: store the URL path (without leading /) into this param. Used for WebSocket paths.

fragment_to.description: url style: store the fragment (#...) into this param. Usually the human-readable remark/name.

raw_to.description: url style: store the entire normalized URI string into this param.

query_map.description: url style: maps query param names to module param names. Example: {"sni": "sni", "pbk": "public_key"}.

json_map.description: base64_json style: maps JSON field names to module param names. Example: {"add": "server_host", "id": "uuid"}.
